meaning: n.库班河(在前苏联北高加索)
examples: The Kuban produces an eighth of Russia's grain, meat, and milk.
The Moscow-based artist, who studied sculpture at Kuban State University, southern Russia, learnt his craft while working at a puppet theatre and even makes the clothes himself.
When asked about the global outlook, Dr. Kuban predicted a total market size of 18-25 GW by the end of 2013, a growth that will for a significant part be stimulated by today's second tier markets.
The striker, who has scored 14 times this season, has been linked with a move to Kuban Krasnodar.
While some midpriced slice providers seem to be flourishing in other boroughs, Manhattan pizzerias "are under siege, " said Adam Kuban, founder of the pizza blog Slice.
